endpoints.name: List calendar events
endpoints.description: Get a list of calendar events
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Create calendar event
endpoints.description: Create a calendar event for a patient. Attribute `all_day` must be set to `true` and `end_at` cannot be set for `plan-check-in` event type.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Get a calendar event
endpoints.description: Get a calendar event by id
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a calendar event
endpoints.description: Delete a calendar event by id
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a calendar event
endpoints.description: Update a calendar event for a patient. Attribute `all_day` must be true and `end_at` cannot be specified for `plan-check-in` event type. To mark a calendar event as 'completed', set `completed_at` and `completed_by` to desired values.  To mark a completed calendar event as 'not completed', set `completed_at` and `completed_by` to `null`. Attendees can be added or removed, but response status cannot be updated. Use the calendar event response api for response status updates instead.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create calendar event response
endpoints.description: Create a calendar event response for an attendee of a calendar event, the attendee can be a coach or patient.  Calendar event responses cannot be fetched, updated nor deleted.  Use calendar event api to fetch the response status for attendees.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: Create an oauth token
endpoints.description:
Create an OAuth 2.0 Bearer token. A valid bearer token is required for all other API requests.

Be sure to set the header `Content-Type: "application/vnd.api+json"`. Otherwise, you will get an error
403 Forbidden. Using `Content-Type: "application/json"` is permitted (to support older oauth clients) but when
using `application/json` the body should have a body in the following format instead of nesting under
`data.attributes`:
```
{
  "grant_type": "client_credentials",
  "client_id": "95c78ab2-167f-40b8-8bec-8398d4b87454",
  "client_secret": "35d18dc9-a3dd-4948-b787-063a490b9354"
}
```
endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.notes: Replace {{TWINEHEALTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your TWINEHEALTH ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.
